steps.title: Hear It In Music
steps.instruction: The 3:2 polyrhythm is one of the most fundamental rhythmic patterns in music. It appears in Afro-Cuban clave, West African drumming, and countless pop, rock, and jazz songs.
steps.secondaryText: Listen for the interplay between the groups of 3 and 2. Once you hear it, you'll start noticing it everywhere.

steps.title: Feel The Shape
steps.instruction: Watch the radial visualizer. Layer A (indigo) plays 3 evenly-spaced beats while Layer B (orange) plays 2. Notice how the beats weave together to create one unified pattern.
steps.secondaryText: Let the shape sink in. There's no rush — just observe.

steps.title: Learn The Mnemonic
steps.instruction: Say this phrase in rhythm to internalize the 3:2 pattern:
steps.secondaryText: The capitalized syllables land on Layer A (the 3). The bold syllables land on Layer B (the 2). 'NOT' lands on both — that's beat 1.

steps.title: Sing The 3
steps.instruction: Listen to just Layer B (the 2-beat pulse). Try singing or tapping the 3-beat pattern on top. Feel how the 3 fits against the 2.
steps.secondaryText: When you feel confident, tap 'I did it!' to move on.

steps.title: Walk + Clap
steps.instruction: Walk in place to the 2-beat pulse (your feet). Clap the 3-beat pattern with your hands. Let your body feel both layers simultaneously.
steps.secondaryText: Keep going for 60 seconds. The timer will count down.

steps.title: Both Hands
steps.instruction: Tap the left zone for Layer A (3 beats) and the right zone for Layer B (2 beats). Try to stay in sync with the audio.
steps.secondaryText: Focus on feeling the groove, not perfection.

steps.title: Disappearing Beat
steps.instruction: The audio will gradually fade away. Can you keep the rhythm going in your head and body?
steps.secondaryText: This is the ultimate test of internalization.
